axes.principle: Overnight Ultrawork needs bounded-or-better autonomy, with headless as the product target.
Long-running agent loops need resumable recovery as the floor and durable checkpoints as the production bar.
Pick the lowest adoption surface that solves the job; internalize patterns before adding external harnesses.

patterns.takeaway: The TUI is the shell; the harness loop, provider wiring, sandboxing, recovery, and extension model carry the product quality.
patterns.superKimiAdoption: Keep user-facing Ultrawork simple while making loop/recovery/tool evidence visible in status and gates.

patterns.takeaway: Use MCP tool discovery and retrieval before loading tool schemas so large tool sets do not bloat every turn.
patterns.superKimiAdoption: Track default exposed tools, schema-token pressure, and on-demand tool retrieval as SOTA gate inputs.

patterns.takeaway: Separate application-owned, harness-owned, and agent-owned memory before designing recall UX.
patterns.superKimiAdoption: Show memory readiness by lane instead of a single vague memory health label.

patterns.takeaway: Measure source edits, terminal/TUI workflows, recovery, memory recall, and tool efficiency with executable traces.
patterns.superKimiAdoption: Keep prompt-only scores auxiliary; require live TUI evidence and internal benchmark traces for SOTA claims.

patterns.takeaway: Regenerate the radar from structured data with allowlisted diffs, freshness checks, and a concise mover report.
patterns.superKimiAdoption: Use a deterministic refresh script before turning external harness trends into Ultrawork product work.
